title: UK: Sustainable Aviation Fuel Bill Set for Lords Committee Stage
content: Members of the House of Lords begin their detailed examination of the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Bill in committee stage on Wednesday 10 December. The Sustainable Aviation Fuel Bill will introduce a revenue certainty mechanism to support the production of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) in the UK. The bill aims to do this by designating a government-owned counterparty to engage in contracts with SAF producers. Line by line examination Committee stage is a thorough examination of the individual parts (clauses) of the bill. Starting from the front of the bill, members work through the clauses in order, considering changes (amendments) to the wording or proposals for new clauses. Members speaking on day one of committee stage will consider amendments (changes) to the bill on subjects including rules and limits on revenue certainty contracts, power-to-liquid fuels (SAF of non-biological origin), reports on UK sustainable fuel production and targets, obligations of airline travel providers, and impact on air travel ticket fares.
author: Analysts Account
